name: node
description: JavaScript runtime. Preferred install path is nvm so versions can be switched per-project without sudo.

installs.notes: Two-step: download the script, show it to the user for review (never pipe curl to bash), then run `bash /tmp/nvm-install.sh`. Afterwards nvm requires a new shell or `source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh`, then `nvm install --lts`.
Simpler but pins one global version; fine if the user doesn't need version switching.
Same two-step review flow as macOS. Distro `nodejs` packages are often years out of date — avoid unless the user asks.
Installs the latest LTS version. A new terminal is needed for PATH to update.

commonFailures: nvm is a shell function, not a binary — it will not appear in PATH or `which` output; the rc file must source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh and the current shell must be restarted or re-sourced.
Installing nvm does not install node: `nvm install --lts` is a required follow-up step.
Ubuntu's default `nodejs` apt package is heavily outdated and may not ship `npm`.
